title: specify plan validate --format json response
description: Validates the JSON response body emitted by `specify plan validate --format json` under the v2 JSON contract (RFC-2 §2). The CLI exits 0 when `passed` is true and 2 (EXIT_VALIDATION_FAILED) otherwise; downstream skills should match on the structured payload rather than the exit code alone.

schema-version.description: JSON contract version (RFC-2 §2.2). Bumped in lockstep with breaking changes to the CLI's JSON payloads.

plan.description: Identity of the plan that was validated.

name.description: Initiative name lifted from `plan.yaml`. Expected to be kebab-case per `schemas/plan/plan.schema.json#/$defs/kebabName`.

path.description: Filesystem path the plan was loaded from (typically `plan.yaml` at the repo root, rendered relative to the caller's cwd).

results.description: Every structural and semantic finding produced by `Plan::validate`. Empty when the plan is clean. Warnings do not affect `passed`; only `error`-level findings cause `passed` to be false.

passed.description: True when no `error`-level result is present. Warnings (e.g. `in-progress` entries with no change-dir) do not flip this to false.

validationResult.description: One finding from `Plan::validate`. See `crates/initiative/src/plan/core.rs::PlanValidationResult` for the canonical producer type.

level.description: Severity bucket. `error` blocks the plan from being used as-is; `warning` is advisory.

code.description: Stable machine-readable identifier (e.g. `duplicate-name`, `dependency-cycle`, `missing-change-dir-for-in-progress`). Skills may pattern-match on this value.

entry.description: Name of the offending plan entry when the finding is entry-local; `null` for plan-wide findings.

message.description: Human-readable rendering of the finding. Not a machine contract — match on `code`.
